risk_findings: obsidian-cli requires a running Obsidian instance and can read, create, append, search, and mutate a local vault.
obsidian-cli developer commands include reload, screenshot, DOM inspection, console inspection, CSS inspection, and eval-style app-context operations.
defuddle requires a global npm CLI install and fetches web pages before optionally writing extracted markdown to a local file.
json-canvas and obsidian-bases are useful open or documented file-format candidates, but they still write local files and require format validation.
obsidian-markdown is portable only as an Obsidian-flavored markdown subset; wikilinks, embeds, properties, and vault rendering must stay explicit.

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The JSON Canvas format is documented and useful beyond one agent, but file writes, ID validation, and Obsidian rendering assumptions need a bounded adapter or merge.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate adaptation proposal required before payload or routing changes

candidate_dispositions.rationale: Obsidian-flavored Markdown guidance is useful, but it must be separated from generic Markdown and made explicit about vault-only syntax.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate adaptation proposal required before payload or routing changes

candidate_dispositions.rationale: Bases are Obsidian-specific YAML files with formulas, views, and validation pitfalls; useful, but not a generic knowledge-management Skill.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate adapter proposal required before payload or routing changes

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The behavior depends on installed CLI state, a focused vault, and local app mutation commands, so it cannot become portable payload without runtime boundary work.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate runtime dependency and permission review required

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The clean-web-page extraction pattern is useful, but npm global install, network fetching, and local file output make it a tool adapter, not immediate curated payload.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate tool dependency and network/write boundary review required

boundary_assertions: candidate decisions are not approved payload

risk_findings: The source is too large for one safe admission decision and must be split into smaller product, analytics, research, GTM, and toolkit batches.
Several skills ask the agent to use web search, read customer or analytics files, generate scripts, run Python, write SQL, or save markdown outputs.
The analytics skills overlap with existing data-analytics capabilities and require metric, denominator, statistical-power, and raw-data handling boundaries.
The execution and PRD skills overlap with existing to-prd, to-issues, triage, shipping-and-launch, review, and routing surfaces.
The toolkit contains legal and privacy policy drafting skills that must stay high-boundary reference or rejection candidates unless legal-disclaimer and jurisdiction controls are strengthened.

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The documented-intent and shipping-artifact patterns are strong, but they should enrich existing review and launch governance rather than become duplicate standalone Skills.
The group is valuable, but it overlaps strongly with runtime data-analytics capabilities and needs strict data-source, SQL execution, and statistical-validation boundaries.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate analytics overlap and runtime-equivalence review required

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The execution templates are useful but mostly refine existing PRD, issue slicing, QA, triage, and launch-note surfaces.
Commercial and strategy guidance is useful for broad coverage, but it must be evidence-aware, current-data-aware, and explicit about advice boundaries before admission.
Discovery guidance can broaden the repository beyond software engineering, but duplicate brainstorming, interview, and dashboard surfaces must be suppressed.
Legal, privacy, and personal-document outputs require stronger high-stakes boundaries than the current source-level wording can provide.
Script generation, SQL generation, and synthetic data generation are useful but must be separated from direct execution, sensitive-data use, and database mutation.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate tool-execution and data-safety review required

boundary_assertions: candidate decisions are not approved payload

risk_findings: The source includes a large asset, reference, script, audio, demo, and export pipeline surface that should not be vendored as a single Skill payload.
It references Playwright screenshots, Python image fetching, local HTTP servers, ffmpeg-style video/audio processing, and Doubao TTS environment configuration.
It is intentionally agent-agnostic in places, but still contains Claude-oriented wording and runtime comparisons that need neutralization.
Brand/logo/image acquisition rules are valuable but must preserve copyright, official-source, user-provided asset, and privacy boundaries.
Watermark, MP4/GIF, voiceover, and bundled media behavior requires explicit consumer/output consent before any release or runtime path.

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The anti-slop design direction, expert review, and prototype workflow guidance is useful if made agent-neutral and scoped to existing design/prototype routes.
The brand asset discipline is valuable, but it must not imply unrestricted logo/image scraping or asset redistribution.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate asset provenance and copyright review required

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The HTML deck, animation, screenshot, and export workflow is practical but depends on scripts, bundled assets, browser automation, media tooling, and output-format assumptions.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate toolchain and asset-pack review required

candidate_dispositions.rationale: The TTS and narration flow references environment configuration, generated audio, and long-form video rendering, so it needs a separate credential, cost, and media-rights review.
Bundled audio, images, demos, and components may be useful examples, but asset-level provenance and size/maintenance cost must be reviewed before redistribution.
candidate_dispositions.next_gate: separate asset inventory and redistribution review required

boundary_assertions: candidate decisions are not approved payload
source text is not redistributed as approved curated payload

next_required_gate: Separate approval is required for any adapted payload diff, release-manifest update, routing projection update, consumer install, local sync, publication, or source redistribution.
